name: Cheese and Dijon Pretzels
recipeInstructions:
1. Reserve 1 cup of flour.
2. In a large bowl, combine the remaining flour (3 and 1/4 cups), sugar, salt, yeast, and cheese.
3. Heat milk and mustard until very warm (about 125°F) and stir in dry ingredients.
4. Mix in enough reserved flour to make a soft dough that does not stick to the bowl. Knead on lightly floured surface until smooth and elastic, about 8 minutes.
5. Cover and let rest on floured surface for 10 minutes.
6. Divide dough into 16 equal pieces.
7. Roll each piece into a 20-inch rope.
8. On lightly greased baking sheets, shape into pretzels; curve ends of each rope to make a circle, cross the ends at the top, twist the ends once and lay over the bottom of each circle.
9. Cover and let rise in a warm, draft-free place for 15 minutes.
10. Brush tops with water and sprinkle with coarse salt and sesame or poppy seeds, if desired.
11. Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for 12 to 15 minutes or until golden. Remove pretzels to cooling racks.
